assertions.title: Canonical tuple output [timestamp, open, high, low, close]

steps.expected: Returns array of 5-element tuples [timestamp, open, high, low, close]
steps.observed: Response is an empty array [] - data available for recent time range shows valid tuple structure
Returns 7 tuples each with [timestamp, open, high, low, close] format: [[1773792000000,73909.37,74672.34,70500,71246.54],...]

evidence.network: GET /coins/bitcoin/ohlc/range?vs_currency=usd&from=1773773889&to=1774378689 -> 200 OK with valid tuple array

steps.action: GET OHLC data and verify timestamp ordering
steps.expected: Each tuple's timestamp > previous tuple's timestamp

evidence.network: Verified 7 consecutive daily data points in strict ascending chronological order

steps.action: Verify all OHLC tuple elements are finite numbers
steps.expected: All 5 tuple elements are numeric and finite (no null, Infinity, NaN)
steps.observed: Sample tuple [1773792000000,73909.37,74672.34,70500,71246.54] - all values are finite numbers

evidence.network: Verified numeric types for all tuple elements across response data

assertions.title: Time range validation (malformed, missing, reversed from/to)

steps.expected: 400 Bad Request with error about invalid from value
steps.observed: HTTP 400 Bad Request with body: {"error":"invalid_parameter","message":"Invalid from value: invalid"}

steps.expected: 400 Bad Request or empty result for invalid range
steps.observed: HTTP 200 OK with empty array [] - gracefully handles reversed range by returning no data

evidence.network: Malformed: 400, Missing from: 400, Missing to: 400, Reversed: 200 (empty)

steps.observed: HTTP 200 OK with daily aggregated tuples
HTTP 400 Bad Request: {"error":"invalid_parameter","message":"Unsupported interval value: 1h"} - API currently only supports daily intervals

steps.expected: 400 Bad Request with clear error message
steps.observed: HTTP 400 Bad Request: {"error":"invalid_parameter","message":"Unsupported interval value: invalid"}

summary: Tested 5 assertions: 5 passed. All OHLC range endpoint behaviors validated: canonical tuple output format, chronological ordering, finite numeric values, time range validation, and interval validation.
